title: Finnish-Inspired Values, Equity and Ethical Positioning: From Principle to Classroom Action
avatar_direction: Professional, warm, practical educator-trainer tone. Speak slowly with clear emphasis on action verbs.

scenes.title: Visual hook
scenes.on_screen_text: Finnish-Inspired Values, Equity and Ethical Positioning
scenes.narration: In this module, we begin with a real professional problem: A centre advertises “official Finnish qualification” without a confirmed Finnish partner and teachers are unsure how to describe the programme to parents. The Finnish-inspired response is not to blame the learner or the teacher. We look first at evidence and access.
scenes.visual_direction: Show a realistic classroom, preschool, or leadership scene. No abstract theory slide.

scenes.title: Principle card
scenes.narration: The key principle is equity. In FCEI training, a principle is useful only when it changes what the educator does next.
scenes.visual_direction: Display one clean keyword card with a simple icon.

scenes.title: Read the evidence
scenes.narration: Before deciding a solution, pause and name the observable evidence. What did learners say, write, do, avoid, misunderstand or complete successfully?
scenes.visual_direction: Show learner work samples, observation notes or a mini-whiteboard evidence check.

scenes.title: Three routes
scenes.on_screen_text: Support Route -> Core Route -> Extension Route
scenes.narration: Start with the Support Route to remove the access barrier. Then protect the Core Route so every learner works toward the shared standard. Finally, add the Extension Route for deeper challenge, transfer and creativity.
scenes.visual_direction: Use a three-lane pathway graphic.

scenes.title: Teacher/leader action
scenes.narration: Try these practical actions: Barrier-first planning: identify the access barrier before changing the expectation.; Visible success criteria: show what good work looks like through models, rubrics or exemplars.; Evidence pause: stop midway to collect learner evidence and adapt before the lesson ends.. The goal is daily improvement, not performance for inspection.
scenes.visual_direction: Show teacher modelling, peer support, route cards and learner choice.

scenes.title: Evidence checkpoint
scenes.narration: Collect one quick piece of evidence before the end. It can be an exit ticket, a learner explanation, a photo of a task, a checklist or a short reflection.
scenes.visual_direction: Show upload button and portfolio evidence sample.

scenes.title: Implementation task
scenes.narration: Your implementation task is to apply one idea from Finnish-Inspired Values, Equity and Ethical Positioning in a real setting and upload evidence with a short reflection. Ask: what improved, what still needs support, and what will I change next?
scenes.visual_direction: End with FCEI logo, download icon and reflective question.
